summary: Small PL subsystem matching slice split from PR #2617. The only loaded source change in src/melee/pl/pltrick.c reorders Fighter* local declarations inside pl_80038144, and the decomp-dev report records a +5 byte improvement for that unmatched item, from 98.26% to 98.73%. The PR body says the slice was isolated by path for review and passed git diff --check; no human review comments were loaded.

key_files.role: Only changed file; local declaration order adjusted in pl_80038144.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: decomp-dev report for main/melee/pl/pltrick pl_80038144

what_changed.change: Moved the declaration of Fighter* attacker_fp after Fighter* victim_fp, leaving the surrounding local declarations otherwise unchanged.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: - Fighter* attacker_fp; before victim_fp; + Fighter* attacker_fp; after victim_fp

what_changed.change: The PR was presented as a directory/subsystem split from PR #2617 scoped to src/melee/pl.

smart_moves.lesson: For broad matching work, splitting by directory/subsystem can make review easier and keep the changed-file surface narrow.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: split from #2617 by directory/subsystem so matching improvements are easier to review

smart_moves.lesson: A tiny local declaration-order edit can be isolated and verified against a single reported unmatched item rather than mixed with broader source-branch changes.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: decomp-dev report lists one improvement in pl_80038144

decomp_lessons.lesson: Declaration order can affect matching for a function even when no executable C statements change; in this slice, reordering Fighter* locals in pl_80038144 corresponded to a reported +5 byte improvement.
decomp_lessons.caveat: The loaded evidence reports an improvement, not a full function match, and does not prove a general declaration-order rule beyond this local case.

naming_conventions.convention: No new naming convention was introduced. The changed declaration block retains role-based Fighter* local names such as attacker_fp and victim_fp.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Fighter* attacker_fp and Fighter* victim_fp in pl_80038144

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Try narrow local declaration-order adjustments when stack/register layout appears to be the remaining mismatch, and keep the evidence tied to the specific symbol.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: decomp-dev report records +5 byte improvement for pl_80038144
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: This should remain evidence-led; the PR does not include assembly details or objdiff output beyond the automated score report.

accepted_candidate_records.title: pl_80038144 declaration-order tweak improved unmatched-item score
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2628, the only loaded code change moved Fighter* attacker_fp after Fighter* victim_fp in src/melee/pl/pltrick.c:pl_80038144. The decomp-dev report recorded a +5 byte improvement for main/melee/pl/pltrick pl_80038144, from 98.26% to 98.73%. Treat this as a local matching tactic example, not a general declaration-order rule.
